target: WIN-063 defense neutralization — close remaining TLDR surfaces that still expose the c5 advocate_mode defense (defense_survives=3). The decider commit 940be96 (2026-05-26T01:22Z) applied c5 H1+H2 to detail_verdict_text and added the H4 axis-vs-magnitude pre-empt to detail_evidence. The same c5 H2 "modeled from first principles" overclaim still survives in tldr_verdict, and the c5 H1 strawman "adopts that value from CHAOS-7 observation rather than deriving it from dome geometry" still survives in tldr_evidence (identical wording to the surface c5 H1 explicitly flagged in tldr_verdict; not separately catalogued because c5 H1 focused on detail_verdict + tldr_verdict). Both TLDR surfaces are high-visibility skim-reader surfaces, so the c5 defense_survives=3 narrative remains constructible from them. Two find/replace patches close both holes; both mirror the dilemma/chain-anchoring framing already accepted into the long-form fields.

state_at_authoring.detail_evidence: OK — c4 May-18 dilemma framing + c5 H4 axis-vs-magnitude pre-empt both present
state_at_authoring.detail_verdict_text: OK — c5 H1 dilemma framing applied AND c5 H2 "reproduced by MHD geodynamo simulations from independently measured CMB boundary conditions" recast applied
state_at_authoring.tldr_verdict: PARTIAL — c5 H1 "provides no published source for that specific value and no derivation from dome geometry" applied, but c5 H2 "modeled from first principles" overclaim SURVIVES in the opening sentence
state_at_authoring.tldr_evidence: BROKEN — c5 H1 strawman wording "adopts that value from CHAOS-7 observation rather than deriving it from dome geometry" survives verbatim; c5 hole list focused on detail_verdict + tldr_verdict and did not catalogue this surface, but it is the identical pattern and is reachable by the same defender quote-our-paragraph attack

decision.rationale: Option A (two targeted find/replace patches, one for tldr_verdict and one for tldr_evidence) is correct because: (1) the c5 review explicitly recommends minor_edit, and the decider has already applied two of the three required parallel-surface fixes; the remaining two are mechanical mirror operations of the framing already accepted into detail_verdict_text and detail_evidence; (2) the defense rated 3 is constructible from any single skim-reader surface that still says "adopts from CHAOS-7" or "first principles" — neutralization requires sweeping ALL surfaces, not just deepening the already-fixed ones; (3) the tldr_verdict and tldr_evidence are exactly the skim-reader surfaces the c5 best_defense paragraph names; (4) a wholesale TLDR rewrite would risk losing the existing accessible-language quality and re-trigger length/style review.
decision.why_not_option_b: Option B (also re-cast the chain-anchoring framing into the tldrs) would be over-scope: TLDRs are 2-3 sentence skim surfaces by design; cramming the four-domain seismic-tomography→CMB-conditions→MHD-output→magnetic-comparison chain into a TLDR sentence trades accessibility for adversarial-proofness in a venue where the adversarial reader will dig into detail_verdict_text anyway. The current TLDRs say enough to be defensible once the strawman wording is removed; the long-form fields carry the chain argument.
decision.why_kernel_of_truth_matters_here: The c5 advocate_mode best_defense correctly identified a real overclaim in our prior text — "derived from first principles via MHD" was an overstatement. Aubert et al. 2013 reproduces qualitative asymmetric decay from CMB heat-flux boundary conditions that themselves come from seismic tomography (PREM/AK135) and mineral-physics constraints on lower-mantle conditions. Calling this "first principles" obscures the chain. The kernel of truth the dome defender exposed: standard physics also accepts observational inputs at some level; the methodological discriminator is not "first principles vs adopted from observation" but how many independent observational domains constrain the model before it meets the comparison dataset. We owe the dome defender intellectual honesty about this; the patches below import that honesty into the TLDRs.

related_prior_expansions.what: Three detail_evidence patches: (P1) four-parameter list, (P2) SAA-shoulder longitudinal-arbitrariness with ~150°E mirror counterfactual, (P3) Olson&Deguen "you accept fitting too" rebuttal-handling paragraph. All integrated. Established the dilemma framing and the independent-input MHD argument that EXP-433 now propagates into the TLDR surfaces.

Applied c5 H1 (CHAOS-7 strawman fix) to detail_verdict_text AND tldr_verdict second sentence; applied c5 H2 (first-principles → independent-CMB-inputs recast) to detail_verdict_text opening; applied c5 H4 (axis-vs-magnitude clarifying sentence) to detail_evidence. Did NOT update tldr_verdict opening (still "modeled from first principles") and did NOT update tldr_evidence (still "adopts that value from CHAOS-7 observation rather than deriving it from dome geometry"). EXP-433 closes both gaps.

patches.find: The asymmetry ratio comes from core fluid dynamics, modeled from first principles.
patches.replace: The asymmetry ratio is reproduced by core-fluid-dynamics simulations from independently measured seismic-tomography inputs (Aubert et al. 2013) — the qualitative asymmetry emerges from independent input data, but the specific 3:1 is not a first-principles prediction in either model.
patches.rationale: Closes c5 H2 in tldr_verdict (currently unaddressed). Mirrors the recast already accepted into detail_verdict_text opening. Replaces the "first principles" overclaim with the chain-anchoring framing that survives the c5 advocate_mode best_defense ("Aubert et al. don't publish a 3:1 ratio — they reproduce asymmetric decay qualitatively from CMB heat-flux boundary conditions that come from seismic tomography. Neither model derives 3:1 from first principles."). The new sentence preserves the verdict thrust (standard physics explains the phenomenon) while removing the rebuttable overstatement. Length impact: +197 chars on this sentence; total tldr_verdict goes from 283 → ~480 chars.

Worth flagging to tinker as a verify-pattern: "parallel-surface fixes" should enumerate ALL fields that contain the target wording, not just the fields named in the hole text.

anticipated_objections.objection: Defender: "Your 'chain-anchoring' framing is just an aesthetic preference for longer derivations — a longer chain has more places to be wrong, not fewer. You're privileging story length over predictive economy."
anticipated_objections.response: Length is not the discriminator; independent anchoring is. Standard physics anchors its derivation in three measurement modalities with three different bias regimes: seismic-tomography (earthquake-wave analysis at seismometer arrays), mineral-physics constraints on lower-mantle conditions (diamond-anvil cell experiments at high P/T), and magnetic-field observations (satellite magnetometers + ground observatories). If any one of these constituent measurements were inconsistent with the others, the whole story would have to revise — and we have decades of cross-checks showing they're consistent. The dome's claim has one observation (CHAOS-7 hemispheric ratio) and one assertion (3:1 = "matches"), with no cross-checking against any independent measurement. Independence of constraint, not length of chain, is what we mean. The argument generalizes: a 2-step chain anchored in 2 independent measurement modalities beats a 5-step chain that loops back on a single dataset.

anticipated_objections.objection: Defender: "You patched the TLDRs to mirror the long-form fields, but you didn't change the verdict. If the verdict (Std Model Explains) is unchanged after admitting 'neither side derives 3:1 from first principles,' wasn't the verdict always wrong?"
anticipated_objections.response: The verdict was correct; the prior verdict-text overstated the basis. "Std Model Explains" refers to the qualitative phenomenon (asymmetric hemispheric decay), which IS explained by reversed-flux geodynamo physics from independent seismic inputs — that explanation has multi-modality empirical support. The specific number 3:1 is a separate matter; the dome's problem on the number is not that it disagrees with a derived prediction (neither model derives it) but that the dome has no independent constraint on what number to expect. Patching the wording from "first principles" to "reproduced from independent inputs" tightens the argument without weakening the verdict. The verdict was right; the supporting text now says the right thing about why.

anticipated_objections.objection: Defender: "raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt is your transcription of our page from 2026-05-08. We've had three weeks since to publish a derivation. Are you sure no derivation has appeared?"
anticipated_objections.response: The poller has verified the WIN-063 page-hash as byte-identical across 34 consecutive polls through 2026-05-25 (curmudgeon WIN-063.c5 dome_code_analysis confirms). No source-file changes (monitor.py, scripts/pull_data.py, inject_ai_layer.py) have been observed in the same window. The transcription is current; if a derivation existed, it would have changed the page hash. If the dome subsequently publishes a derivation, this WIN gets re-reviewed in the normal change-detection cycle and the verdict can be revisited.

kernel_of_truth: The c5 advocate_mode best_defense did real work. The phrase "derived from first principles via MHD geodynamo simulations" in our prior detail_verdict_text was an overclaim: Aubert et al. 2013 reproduces the asymmetry from observational seismic-tomography inputs, not from first principles alone. A dome defender quoting Aubert et al.'s methods section (which is explicit about using PREM/AK135 + mantle-tomography boundary conditions) would have caught us in exactly the same kind of "adopt-from-observation, relabel-as-derived" move we're faulting the dome for on the 3:1 ratio. The c5 review correctly named this overclaim; the decider correctly applied the recast to detail_verdict_text; and the TLDR patches in this EXP propagate the correction to the surfaces a skim reader would actually see. Owning this overclaim publicly (by updating our text instead of defending it) is what gives us standing to fault the dome for asymmetric-treatment of fitting-vs-derivation. The kernel was: the dome defender noticed our verbal sleight-of-hand. We acknowledge it and fix it.

(2) Verify tldr_verdict length 283 → ~480 chars is within tolerance for the tldr_verdict_length test (typical bound is 500). If the test fails, P1 can be shortened by dropping the parenthetical "(Aubert et al. 2013)" attribution — the long-form fields already carry the citation. (3) Verify tldr_evidence length 554 → ~613 chars is within tolerance for the tldr_evidence_length test (typical bound is 700). (4) Manual cross-check: grep the surviving page-hash baseline (raw-text/02b-wins-V51-win064.txt:12) to confirm the dome paragraph still contains zero "CHAOS-7" occurrences — if the dome publishes a derivation between authoring and integration, the dilemma framing needs revisiting before the patch ships.

(b) Push WIN-063 to curmudgeon for c6 verification review (queue_id auto-assigned, class=verification) — the c5 review explicitly recommended a c6 to verify the rated-3 defense no longer survives once all parallel surfaces are patched. (c) Re-open ISS-2120 OR create a successor issue tracking "parallel-surface fix sweep" pattern for future c-cycle audits — the c5 deeper_analysis flagged that ISS-2120 was closed prematurely because the May-18 fix was scoped to detail_evidence only; the same lesson applies to the c5 fix scope. (d) Optional: file a small tinker note about the verify-pattern observation in new_evidence_added — when applying "parallel-surface fixes," the decider should enumerate ALL fields containing the target wording, not just the fields explicitly named in the hole text.
scope_note: EXP-433 issue_ids field carries a placeholder "ISS-2201-tracker-ref" because the source tracker entry references ISS-2201 — but ISS-2201 is actually closed and is about WIN-020 c6 (Saros/nodal conflation), not WIN-063. This appears to be a decider tracker-write error (likely a queue_id / ISS-id mix-up around the same timestamp). The substantive work is unambiguous from the tracker entry's target field, curmudgeon_review reference, and category=defense_neutralization. Recommend the decider clean up the issue_ids field at integration time to point at the actual ISS-2120 (re-opened) or a successor ISS-NNNN tracking the parallel-surface sweep.
tldr: WIN-063 c5 defense_survives=3: the decider commit 940be96 fixed the dilemma framing in detail_verdict_text and the chain-anchoring recast in detail_verdict_text, but two TLDR surfaces still expose the same defense — tldr_verdict still says "modeled from first principles" (c5 H2 overclaim) and tldr_evidence still says "adopts that value from CHAOS-7 observation rather than deriving it from dome geometry" (c5 H1 strawman). Two find/replace patches close both. Verdict (Std Model Explains) unchanged.
